title: oreocarya holoptera
parts: Oreocarya holoptera has many medicinal parts, such as its roots, which contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and alkaloids, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and antiseptic activities. The flowers of Oreocarya holoptera contain volatile oils, including limonene and pinene, which have been used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation. The entire plant, including its stems and leaves, has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, with its leaves and roots often being used separately for different medicinal purposes.
side_effects: Oreocarya holoptera can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. In extreme cases, excessive consumption may lead to kidney damage and increased blood pressure. Additionally, the plant's high content of certain compounds may interact with medications, resulting in adverse reactions, such as allergic reactions, dizziness, and headaches. If not used under the guidance of a medical professional, Oreocarya holoptera can cause long-term health problems.
